violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2025

Chili which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 107 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025
The dumpster enclosure has an accumulation of leaf litter and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Chili was observed held at 111 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Hot holding unit was observed off. PIC turned on unit and chili was reheated to 174 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

The door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

Door to 1 door low boy refrigerator does not close all the way. Gaskets also observed in poor repair. Unit taken out of service during inspection. Establishment not to place any TCS foods until unit is repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: 1. Under drive thru counters 2. Under fryers 3. Under booth seating cushions (rodent droppings) The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023
Handle to women's restroom was observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023
